title: Taking Turns
lesson: Waiting and taking turns means everyone gets a chance to play.
prompts: In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY} sits on the swing seat, both hands gripping the two chains and legs stretched forward mid-swing, beaming happily toward two approaching friends. {GENDER_F_AGE_06} walks up across the grass from the right, her right hand raised in an open-palm wave, smiling warmly. {GENDER_M_AGE_06} walks across the grass beside her, his right hand raised in an open-palm wave, grinning happily. The girl's wave and the boy's wave both point toward the swinging child, and the child looks back toward the two of them. Exactly three children in the frame, and no other people.

In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY} sits on the swing seat gripping the two chains, leaning back with a surprised, wide-eyed expression. {GENDER_F_AGE_06} stands close on the left side of the occupied swing, her hand clutching the left chain, leaning in eagerly with an impatient expression. {GENDER_M_AGE_06} stands close on the right side of the swing, his hand clutching the right chain, leaning in eagerly with an impatient expression. The girl's hand grips the left chain and the boy's hand grips the right chain of the one swing the seated child is on, and both their gazes are fixed on that swing. Exactly three children in the frame, and no other people.

In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY} stands at the left, turned to the right toward two friends, one open hand raised gently at shoulder height as if suggesting they wait their turn, with a calm, friendly smile. {GENDER_F_AGE_06} stands back from the swing on the right, turned to face the child, one open hand lifted in a small conversational gesture, with a patient, attentive expression. {GENDER_M_AGE_06} stands beside her on the right, turned to face the child, one open hand lifted in a small conversational gesture, with a patient, attentive expression. The child's raised open hand and gaze aim toward the girl and the boy, while the girl's gaze and open hand and the boy's gaze and open hand aim back toward the child. The child, the girl, and the boy are three separate, distinct children — no fourth child, no duplicate or extra child, no twin. Exactly three children in the frame, and no other people.

In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY} stands to one side clapping both hands together, with a cheerful, encouraging expression. {GENDER_F_AGE_06} sits on the swing seat gripping the two chains, legs stretched forward mid-swing with a delighted grin. {GENDER_M_AGE_06} stands close behind the swing, both hands pressed gently against the girl's back to push her, with an excited smile. The boy's hands rest on the girl's back and his gaze follows the swing, while the standing child looks toward the swinging girl. Exactly three children in the frame, and no other people.

In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY} sits on the swing seat, both hands gripping the two chains and legs stretched forward mid-swing, beaming with a joyful smile. {GENDER_F_AGE_06} stands close behind the swing, both hands pressed gently against the swinging child's back to push, with an encouraging smile. {GENDER_M_AGE_06} stands to one side clapping his hands, with a delighted, cheering expression as he waits his turn. The girl's hands rest on the swinging child's back and her gaze follows the swing, while the boy looks toward the swinging child. Exactly three children in the frame, and no other people.

In a cheerful sunny playground beside a single swing — one swing seat hanging by two chains from a wooden A-frame, soft grass, warm sunlight — three children stand in one straight left-to-right row, evenly spaced with a small gap of grass between neighbors and joined only by their clasped hands, all laughing with big happy smiles. From left to right: first {GENDER_F_AGE_06}, then the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY} in the middle, then {GENDER_M_AGE_06}. The girl's right hand clasps the middle child's left hand, and the middle child's right hand clasps the boy's left hand. The girl, the child, and the boy stand apart as three separate figures — exactly one girl and not two, no fourth child, no duplicate or extra child, no twin. Exactly three children in the frame, and no other people.
negative_prompts: two children sitting on one swing, a second child seated on the swing seat, a friend sitting on the child's lap, extra swing, second swing, multiple swings
fourth child, extra child, duplicate child, twin, more than three children, extra person in the background, crowd of children
fourth child, extra child, duplicate child, twin, second girl, two girls, more than three children, extra person in the background, crowd of children
gists: At a sunny playground, the child swings happily on the single swing while two friends, a girl and a boy, walk up and wave hello — the child is enjoying the swing.
By the swing, the child sits on the swing looking surprised as the two friends grab its chains from either side, each wanting the one swing at once — everyone wants the same swing.
By the swing, the child raises one hand calmly suggesting they wait their turn while the two friends stand back and discuss the idea with patient, attentive expressions.
By the swing, the child claps and cheers as the girl swings with a delighted grin and the boy gently pushes her from behind — a friend takes the first turn.
By the swing, the child swings with a joyful smile while the girl gently pushes from behind and the boy waits beside cheering — it's the child's turn, and the friends help and wait.
By the swing, the child and the two friends hold hands in a row, all happy — everyone got a turn and had fun.
texts: {NAME} was having a wonderful time on the swing — up and down, up and down! Two friends came over. "Hi, {NAME}!" they called, waving. "Watch me go!" laughed {NAME}.
But the friends wanted the swing too. "My turn now!" said the girl, grabbing a chain. "No, me first!" said the boy, grabbing the other. The swing wobbled to a stop. "Wait!" said {NAME}.
"Let's take turns," said {NAME} gently, raising a hand. "Good idea," said the girl, and the friends stepped back and nodded.
The girl went first — wheee! "Higher, higher!" cheered {NAME}, clapping, while the boy gave a gentle push. "This is so much fun!" laughed the girl.
Then it was {NAME}'s turn! The girl gave a gentle push. "Wheee!" laughed {NAME}, swinging up high. "You go so high!" cheered the boy, waiting beside for his turn.
Everyone got a turn, and everyone had fun. "That was the best!" said {NAME}. They all held hands, as happy as can be. Taking turns means everyone gets to play.
